task_text: Open project_helios_status.txt and reformat it so that each section has a bold heading, bullet points replace any run-on paragraphs, and the file is saved back in place. Do not change any of the factual content.

content.text: Sure! Let me start by reading the file so I know exactly what content I'm working with.

content.content:
**Project Helios — Weekly Status Update**
- Week of 2026-07-06

**Overall**
- ON TRACK (minor risks)

**Milestone Progress**
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

**Sprint 14 Goals**
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

**Open Blockers**
- 1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
  - Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
- 2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
  - Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

**Next Milestone**
- Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

content.text:
The file is already well-formatted — it has bold headings, bullet points throughout, and no run-on paragraphs. Every section already uses the structure you've described:

- **Bold headings** (e.g., `**Overall**`, `**Sprint 14 Goals**`, etc.) ✅
- **Bullet points** replacing any prose (including the blockers with sub-bullets) ✅
- **No run-on paragraphs** — all content is already in list form ✅

Because the file is already in the requested format, rewriting it would risk introducing unintended changes to the factual content, which you specifically asked to avoid. **No changes are needed** — the file is already correctly structured.

If you'd like me to make any specific additional adjustments (e.g., numbering, indentation style, section order), just let me know!
